user.title: Add an LLM Middleman backend
user.description: Choose the backend type this integration connects to. Each conversation agent is added afterwards as a subentry.

data_description.backend_type: The external backend protocol this connection speaks.

openai_compat.title: OpenAI-compatible connection
openai_compat.description: Connect to an OpenAI-compatible chat-completions server.

data_description.base_url: Server root address without any path. The /v1 path is added automatically, so a trailing /v1 (or slash) is stripped.
data_description.api_key: Optional. Many "OpenAI-compatible" servers still require a non-empty dummy key.

langgraph.title: LangGraph connection
langgraph.description: Connect to a LangGraph deployment (langgraph dev, self-hosted, or cloud).

data_description.base_url: LangGraph deployment URL (a trailing slash is stripped automatically).
data_description.api_key: Optional; sent as the x-api-key header.

ollama.title: Ollama connection
ollama.description: Connect to an Ollama server's native API.

data_description.base_url: Ollama host root, e.g. http://host:11434 — not the /v1 path (a trailing slash is stripped automatically).

converse.title: Custom /v1/converse connection
converse.description: Connect to a backend speaking the custom /v1/converse contract.

data_description.base_url: Base URL of the backend; the /v1/converse path is appended (a trailing slash is stripped automatically).
data_description.token: Optional bearer token sent as an Authorization header.

n8n.title: n8n connection
n8n.description: Connect to an n8n Chat Trigger or plain Webhook workflow.

data_description.webhook_url: Full production chat URL (…/webhook/ID/chat). Stored verbatim — it is opaque and never normalized.
data_description.target_type: Chat Trigger, or plain Webhook with a Respond to Webhook node.
data_description.username: Basic-auth username (when authentication is basic auth).
data_description.password: Basic-auth password (when authentication is basic auth).
data_description.header_name: Header name (when authentication is a custom header).
data_description.header_value: Header value (when authentication is a custom header).

dify.title: Dify connection
dify.description: Connect to a Dify chat, agent, or chatflow app. The app's prompt, model, and tools are configured inside Dify itself and are not set here.

data_description.base_url: Dify API root including /v1, e.g. https://api.dify.ai/v1 (a trailing slash is stripped, and /v1 is appended if you omit it).
data_description.api_key: The Dify app's API key (starts with app-). Required.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect. Check the URL and that the backend is reachable.

data_description.name: Display name for this agent's conversation entity.
data_description.prompt: Instructions the agent is given (a template). Sent as the system prompt to backends that accept one.
data_description.model: Model this agent uses. Pick one the backend offers, or type a name.
data_description.max_history: How many past turns to replay to stateless backends. Higher costs more per turn; full-replay backends may ignore it.
data_description.timeout: Per-turn deadline. The pipeline falls back rather than hang past this.
data_description.memory_scope: Which conversations share memory on stateful backends: per conversation, per device, or one continuous thread for the agent.
data_description.llm_hass_api: Home Assistant tool APIs the backend may call to control devices and query state. Leave empty to disable device control for this agent.
